chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 1: The Alignment Crisis
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the first chapter of the IVD book. Establishes the
# problem: misalignment in traditional and AI-assisted development.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_1_foundations.chapters[0]
# Version: 2.3
# Created: 2026-01-25
# Status: Complete
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Establish the alignment crisis in the AI Agents era: prompts, PRDs, and user stories are not verifiable by AI, leading to many turns and hallucinations. Set up the core question that IVD answers."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Understands the core pain point of the AI Agents era: many turns and hallucinations.
    2. Sees why traditional artifacts (PRDs, user stories, prompts) fail with AI—they are not verifiable by the AI.
    3. Understands the scale: the $10B problem, 70–90% knowledge loss, hundreds of billions in waste.
    4. Feels the pain through concrete examples (AI fills gaps, you correct, repeat).
    5. Asks: "What if intent was verifiable by the AI?"—the hook into Chapter 2.
    
    This chapter does NOT yet present IVD. It names the problem: AI agents guess, and guessing is hallucination.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can restate the "Prompt → Here's X → No, I meant..." pattern in their own words
    - Reader understands why PRDs, user stories, and prompts fail with AI (not verifiable)
    - Reader knows the terms "many turns" and "hallucinations" as the core pain points
    - Chapter ends with "What if intent was verifiable by the AI?" as a natural bridge to Ch2
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1 purpose: establish the problem)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "1.1"
      title: "The Expensive Pattern: \"Build X\" → \"Here's X\" → \"No, I meant...\""
      required_content:
        - "Concrete illustration of the request–deliver–correction loop"
        - "Why it is expensive (time, trust, compounding)"
        - "Applies to human–human and human–AI; AI as amplifier"
    
    - id: "1.2"
      title: "Why Traditional Artifacts Fail: PRDs, User Stories, and Prompts"
      required_content:
        - "PRDs, user stories, prompts are prose—not verifiable by AI"
        - "The AI reads prose and fills in gaps (guesses = hallucinations)"
        - "These artifacts were designed for h

- id: "1.1"
      title: "The Expensive Pattern: \"Build X\" → \"Here's X\" → \"No, I meant...\""
      required_content:
        - "Concrete illustration of the request–deliver–correction loop"
        - "Why it is expensive (time, trust, compounding)"
        - "Applies to human–human and human–AI; AI as amplifier"
    
    - id: "1.2"
      title: "Why Traditional Artifacts Fail: PRDs, User Stories, and Prompts"
      required_content:
        - "PRDs, user stories, prompts are prose—not verifiable by AI"
        - "The AI reads prose and fills in gaps (guesses = hallucinations)"
        - "These artifacts were designed for humans, not AI agents"
    
    - id: "1.3"
      title: "The AI Amplification Effect: Many Turns, Many Hallucinations"
      required_content:
        - "AI agents produce fast, plausible, but wrong output (hallucinations)"
        - "Core pain points: many turns (back-and-forth) and hallucinations (AI fills gaps)"
        - "The cost compounds: context overload, exhaustion, distrust"
        - "Industry statistics: 70–90% knowledge loss, $260B+ annual waste"
    
    - id: "1.4"
      title: "The Core Question: What If Intent Was Verifiable?"
      required_content:
        - "Pose the question as the pivot into IVD"
        - "Do not answer it here; Ch2 does"
        - "One clear, quotable formulation (writing_style.quotable_lines)"
  
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "1–2 quotable, tweetable lines (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "No IVD solution yet—problem only"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Traditional artifacts (PRDs, user stories, prompts) fail with AI—not verifiable by the AI"
  - "The AI Agents era pain points: many turns and hallucinations (AI fills gaps with guesses)"
  - "The scale: 70–90% knowledge loss, hundreds of billions in annual waste"
  - "The hinge: what if intent was verifiable by the AI? What if the AI wrote the intent?"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 memorable, tweetable lines (e.g. in 1.4 or 1.1)"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is quotable on its own"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_1"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    val

mmary' block"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 memorable, tweetable lines (e.g. in 1.4 or 1.1)"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is quotable on its own"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_1"
    requirement: "Prose leads; examples illustrate. Mentor-like, direct, second person. High depth, high accessibility."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style (voice, tone, code_and_prose.part_1_3_4)"
    validation: "Manual: voice and tone match"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms and Key Points, italic for emphasis and dialogue, code formatting for technical terms, proper paragraph structure (3-6 sentences typical, 1-2 for emphasis, 7-10 for dense exposition, >10 MUST be broken with subheadings or split), footnotes with blank lines between."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions (bold/italic/code usage, NO paragraphs >10 sentences without subheadings, footnote spacing)"
  
  - name: "evidence_for_case_study"
    requirement: "Statistics and claims in 1.3 must be sourced or marked composite/illustrative; no invented, unsourced statistics"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.evidence_based"
    validation: "1.3 has inline footnotes with attribution; any $/numbers traceable or labeled"
  
  - name: "author_sourced_examples"
    requirement: "Concrete examples (1.1 CSV scenario, 1.3 industry data) must derive from the author's direct experience (anonymized) or be explicitly labeled as industry-typical/composite. Prefer author's experience. Pattern: research 6.1 'Author experience and case studies'; book_system_intent.constraints.author_sourced_examples"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.author_sourced_examples; research/best_selling_programming_books.md 6.1"
    validation: "1.1 example labeled 'industry-typical'; 1.3 statistics have footnotes; no unanchored fiction"
  
  - name: "no_solution_yet"
    requirement: "Do not present IVD or intent artifacts as the solution in this chapter; problem only. The phrase 'what if intent was verifiable' (1.4) is allowed—it is the question, not the answer."
    source: "book_system_intent.book_structure (Ch1 sets up; Ch2 introduces IVD)"
    validation: "No exposition of IVD, intent artifacts, or the eight principles; 1.4 may use 'intent' and 'verifiable' in the question"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 1 establishes the problem only; IVD enters in Chapter 2."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part I purpose (book_system_intent): "Establish the problem and the IVD paradigm; reader understands why intent is primary (theory-first)."
    - First-mover, problem-first structure: like Code Complete (foundation = the prob

on: "No exposition of IVD, intent artifacts, or the eight principles; 1.4 may use 'intent' and 'verifiable' in the question"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 1 establishes the problem only; IVD enters in Chapter 2."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part I purpose (book_system_intent): "Establish the problem and the IVD paradigm; reader understands why intent is primary (theory-first)."
    - First-mover, problem-first structure: like Code Complete (foundation = the problem) and many bestsellers. Reader must feel the pain before the cure.
    - The "What if intent was verifiable?" (1.4) is the hinge: it creates desire for Ch2 without spoiling the paradigm shift.
    - Concrete examples in 1.1–1.3 provide credibility; pattern from bestsellers: establish problem with relatable scenarios.
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_1_foundations.purpose, book_structure); research/best_selling_programming_books.md (structure, case study)"
  date: "2026-01-25"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Chapter stays too abstract without concrete AI-era examples"
    action: "Ensure 1.1–1.3 each have at least one concrete scenario (prompt → wrong output → correction loop)"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'did you feel the pain?'"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Statistics (70–90% knowledge loss, $260B+ waste) challenged or outdated"
    action: "All numbers must have inline attribution or be labeled 'estimated'; update if better data emerges"
    monitor: "Section 1.3 citations"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-01-alignment-crisis/chapter_01_alignment_crisis_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-1-foundations/chapter-01-alignment-crisis/chapter.md"
  status: "complete"
  version: 2
  
  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process.yaml"
    current_step: "step_7_complete"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"
  

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (1.1–1.4) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - che

- "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"
  

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (1.1–1.4) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary and 1–2 quotable lines"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, quotable_principles"
    - check: "Statistics in 1.3 have footnotes with attribution; CSV example in 1.1 labeled industry-typical"
      validates: "constraints.evidence_for_case_study"
    - check: "Concrete examples (1.1, 1.3) grounded in author experience or explicitly labeled industry-typical/composite; no unanchored fiction"
      validates: "constraints.author_sourced_examples"
    - check: "No IVD solution in Ch1 (only the question in 1.4)"
      validates: "constraints.no_solution_yet"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "2.3"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Full manuscript-vs-intent cross-check. Findings and fixes:
      MANUSCRIPT:
      (1) Section 1.4 violated `no_solution_yet`: described intent artifacts, stated "the AI writes the
          intent, not you", described the IVD workflow and outcome. Rewritten to stop at the question
          ("what if intent was verifiable?") without exposing the mechanism. Creates tension for Ch2.
      (2) Key Points bullet 4 named "Intent-Verified Development" — removed; constraint forbids IVD
          exposition in Ch1. Now ends with "The next chapter explores what happens when intent becomes
          verifiable."
      INTENT:
      (3) Constraints `evidence_for_case_study` and `author_sourced_examples` referenced "Case study (1.4)"
          but the case study was removed in v2.0 and absorbed into 1.1–1.3. Updated references to correct
          sections (1.1 CSV example, 1.3 statistics).
      (4) Verification cross_check items referencing "Case study (1.4)" updated to reference 1.1 and 1.3.
    reason: "Manuscript cross-check: fix constraint violations and stale references from v2.0 section restructure."
  - version: "2.2"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Cross-check against book_system_intent v1.5, research, and outline:
      (1) Header Status "Pending" → "Complete" (manuscript exists, implementation.status was already complete).
      (2) Added parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml" (template requirement for non-system intents).
      (3) Synced master intent Ch1 key_points and section titles to match v2.1 AI-era reframe (master was stale).
      (4) Removed duplicate changelog key inside implementation block (YAML spec: second key silently overwrites fir

s and stale references from v2.0 section restructure."
  - version: "2.2"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Cross-check against book_system_intent v1.5, research, and outline:
      (1) Header Status "Pending" → "Complete" (manuscript exists, implementation.status was already complete).
      (2) Added parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml" (template requirement for non-system intents).
      (3) Synced master intent Ch1 key_points and section titles to match v2.1 AI-era reframe (master was stale).
      (4) Removed duplicate changelog key inside implementation block (YAML spec: second key silently overwrites first).
      (5) Fixed verification references: "1.1–1.5" → "1.1–1.4", "question in 1.5" → "question in 1.4" (sections renumbered in v2.0).
      (6) Added risks section (template standard; missing from chapter intent).
    reason: "Alignment audit: chapter intent must match master intent, research, outline, and template conventions."
  - version: "2.1"
    date: "2026-01-26"
    change: "Major reframe: IVD is for the AI Agents era. Updated intent, goal, key_points, and deliverables to focus on: many turns, hallucinations, AI writes intent. Removed 'PM/dev' framing in favor of 'you prompt AI'."
    reason: "IVD is NOT for traditional PM > Developer workflows. IVD is the framework for AI agents to write, implement, and verify intent. Without this, IVD is just another artifact like PRDs."
  - version: "2.0"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    change: "Removed section 1.4 (separate case study) as redundant; concrete examples in 1.1–1.3 sufficient. Renumbered 1.5 → 1.4. Updated goal, success_metric, deliverables, constraints, and verification."
    reason: "Structural simplification; case study content absorbed into sections 1.1–1.3."
  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    change: "1.4 required_content: prefer author's direct experience; composite only when labeled. New constraint author_sourced_examples."
    reason: "Case study must not be unanchored fiction; author credibility (research 6.1)."
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-01-25"
    change: "Initial chapter intent; derived from book/book_system_intent.yaml"
    reason: "IVD: intent before implementation; one intent per chapter folder"
